channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump says federal government should 'take over' DC, backing congressional GOP push
item.description: President Donald Trump is throwing his support behind congressional efforts for a federal takeover of the nation’s capital, saying he approves putting the District of Columbia back under direct federal control.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Hegseth orders Pentagon to make plans for major budget cuts to align with Trump DOGE priorities
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth has ordered the Pentagon to develop plans for cutting 8% from the Department of Defense budget.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ninth Circuit rejects Trump's bid to reinstate birthright citizenship order
item.description: A federal appeals court on Wednesday denied the Trump administration's emergency request for relief from an injunction that blocks it from enforcing Trump's executive order on birthright citizenship.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Florida GOP pushes for government efficiency with DOGE-inspired legislation
item.description: President Donald Trump's Department of Government Efficiency, also known as DOGE, has stirred up controversy as it searches for wasteful federal spending. Now, Florida GOP lawmakers are planning a similar initiative.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Florida Democrats file bill to repeal 6-week abortion ban
item.description: House Minority Leader Fentrice Driskell, who represents families in Hillsborough County, said the legislation comes because it’s a mandate from voters.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump administration halts legal aid for migrant children, leaving some to navigate courts alone
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — Children so young their feet can't touch the floor when they sit in courtroom chairs have been left without representation in immigration court because of Trump administration cuts, according to organizations that help these kids navigate the legal system when they arrive in the U.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, GISELA SALOMON and ELLIOT SPAGAT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Politicians demand cuts to 9/11 health program be reversed
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – The 9/11 health program is taking a heavy hit from Elon Musk's Department of Government Efficiency. Twenty percent of the program's staff were just fired or took buyouts. Already strained workers will now be slower in processing the sick, with far greater wait times and delays.
item.dc:creator: John Muller, PIX 11

item.title: Senate GOP dismayed by Trump, Zelensky war of words
item.description: Public comments from President Trump blaming Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky for Russia’s invasion of his country are unnerving Senate Republicans, who have largely sought to avoid conflicts with the White House.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge homes in on DOGE staffer duties in privacy lawsuit
item.description: A federal judge on Wednesday looked to pinpoint the exact duties of Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) staffers during a hearing over whether to cut off their unfettered access to troves of sensitive personal data. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New York rep. says Trump has committed 'dozens of impeachable offenses' so far
item.description: “Donald Trump has, in his first month, probably committed dozens of impeachable offenses,” Rep. Dan Goldman (D-N.Y.) said in a recent interview.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: State Department makes 5 drug cartels terrorist organization
item.description: Tren de Aragua, MS-13 and the Sinaloa and Jalisco cartels are among those named terrorists by the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Ali Bradley, NewsNation

item.title: Lawmaker who led state to require ethnic studies in schools decries effort to undo it
item.description: State Rep. Samantha Sencer-Mura, DFL-Minneapolis, authored the bill that set ethnic studies standards a few years ago. She joined Morning Edition host Cathy Wurzer on Wednesday in response to Republican Rep. Ron Kresha’s legislation.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: Cuts to Veterans Affairs made in 'dishonest' way: Former employee
item.description: Employees fired included researchers looking into cancer treatments, opioid addiction and burn pit exposure.
item.dc:creator: Nichole Berlie, NewsNation

item.title: SCOOP: Top GOP Sen. Cotton to meet with embattled Trump defense nominee as doubts swirl
item.description: Sen. Tom Cotton, R-Ark., will meet with a key Trump nominee after finding himself at apparent odds with some figures in the MAGA-sphere.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Some of the 400 jobs that were cut at the FAA helped support air safety, a union says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump's administration has said no one at the Federal Aviation Administration with a “critical safety” position has been fired as it cuts the federal workforce, but some FAA jobs that were eliminated had direct roles in supporting safety inspectors and airport oper
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Meet Dale E. Ho, the second-year judge weighing the future of NYC Mayor Eric Adams' corruption case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York Mayor Eric Adams’ lawyer asked, top officials in President Donald Trump's Justice Department agreed, and seven prosecutors resigned in protest. Now, it’s up to Dale E. Ho, a second-year federal judge in Manhattan, to decide if Adams' corruption case goes away.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bird flu response personnel caught in federal workforce cuts
item.description: The firings come amid the Trump administration’s sweeping push to reduce the size of the government and cut the federal workforce.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Trump warns Zelenskyy to quickly negotiate war's end with Russia or risk not having a nation to lead
item.description: President Donald Trump has warned Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy that he “better move fast” to negotiate an end to Russia’s invasion of Ukraine or risk not having a nation to lead.
item.dc:creator: Stephany Matat And Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: National Science Foundation fires roughly 10% of its workforce
item.description: NSF fired 168 employees, leaving the agency less equipped to fund a wide range of scientific research.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Lambert, MPR News

item.title: Trump wants to lower the cost of IVF. A new executive order seeks ideas to do that
item.description: President Trump campaigned on lowering the cost of IVF, after a controversial ruling made the treatment stop in Alabama for a short time.
item.dc:creator: Selena Simmons-Duffin and Diane Webber, MPR News

item.title: DOJ and NYC mayor face judge's scrutiny in bid to dismiss criminal charges
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Justice Department lawyers and New York City Mayor Eric Adams are set to face a federal judge who is signaling that he’s unlikely to rubber stamp their request to drop the mayor's corruption charges weeks before an April trial. Judge Dale E.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump's envoy arrives in Kyiv as US policy shifts toward Russia
item.description: Keith Kellogg, the U.S. special envoy for Ukraine and Russia, arrived in Kyiv on Wednesday for talks with President Volodymyr Zelenskyy and military commanders, as the U.S. shifted its policy away from years of efforts to isolate Russian President Vladimir Putin.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US top diplomat Rubio visits the UAE after landmark talks with Russia over Ukraine war
item.description: U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio has met with the leader of the United Arab Emirates, wrapping up an overseas trip that saw the highest-level outreach between the United States and Russia since Moscow launched its full-scale invasion of Ukraine in 2022.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Lee, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Emotional testimony for 'Jaya's Law' argues for tougher penalties for wrong-way drivers
item.description: A Las Vegas family made an emotional plea to state lawmakers on Tuesday, telling the story of 3-year-old Jaya Brooks, who was killed in a crash involving a wrong-way driver in December 2023.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Senate confirms Howard Lutnick as Commerce secretary
item.description: The Senate on Wednesday confirmed Howard Lutnick to serve as Commerce secretary, approving a key member of President Trump’s economic team amid rising trade tensions. Senators voted 51-45 in favor of Lutnick’s confirmation to lead the Commerce Department, which has broad authority over U.S.
item.dc:creator: Sylvan Lane, NewsNation

item.title: Trump signals 25 percent tariffs coming on cars, semiconductors and pharmaceuticals
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday indicated he would unveil tariffs on imports of automobiles, semiconductors and pharmaceuticals in early April, starting at 25 percent.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 2 Tampa Bay sheriffs to advise Florida cabinet on immigration enforcement
item.description: Two Tampa Bay area sheriffs were tapped to join the newly created State Immigration Enforcement Council on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Judge refuses to block DOGE’s access to student borrower data
item.description: A judge declined to block Elon Musk’s DOGE from gaining access to Education Department data on student borrowers.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Mexico’s President Sheinbaum threatens to take legal action over the Gulf name change
item.description: Earlier this month, Sheinbaum shared a letter addressed to Google with reporters, arguing that the U.S. had no authority to unilaterally rename the Gulf.
item.dc:creator: Manuela López Restrepo, MPR News

item.title: Former Vice President Kamala Harris to receive NAACP's Chairman's Award
item.description: Former Vice President Kamala Harris will receive the NAACP's Chairman's Award during the civil rights organization’s Image Awards ceremony this weekend. The Chairman's Award honors individuals who excel in public service and leverage their unique platforms to ignite and drive meaningful change.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: RFK Jr. rescinds Indian Health Service layoffs
item.description: The last-minute decision to rescind the layoff order for IHS came as health care workers waited for bad news.
item.dc:creator: MPR News

item.title: Activist Curtis Sliwa joins race for NYC mayor
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – The New York City mayoral race just got even more crowded. The founder of the Guardian Angels nonprofit, Curtis Sliwa, has officially filed paperwork to run for the city's top job. He also ran for mayor back in 2021 and lost to the current Mayor Eric Adams.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Proposed bill looks to make President Trump’s birthday a federal holiday
item.description: Rep. Claudia Tenney (R-N.Y.) announced Friday a proposed bill, the Establishment Act, which seeks to make President Trump’s birthday and Flag Day a federal holiday in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Daesha Gear, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump official considering running for California governor if Kamala Harris enters race
item.description: Richard Grenell, a Trump administration official, said Friday that he would consider entering the 2026 California governor race if former Vice President and Democratic Presidential candidate Kamala Harris joins the growing list of candidates.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Nevada joins lawsuit challenging Elon Musk's executive power
item.description: Democratic Nevada Attorney General Aaron Ford joined more than a dozen other attorneys general Thursday in filing a lawsuit challenging Elon Musk's executive power.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: OPM directs agencies to fire government workers still on probation
item.description: Office of Personnel Management (OPM) officials met with agency leaders across government Thursday and directed them to begin firing employees still in their probationary period a year or more after being hired.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Could Kamala Harris be the next California governor? Here's how she's polling
item.description: Former Vice President and Democratic Presidential candidate Kamala Harris holds a substantial lead over other potential candidates to succeed California Gov. Gavin Newsom in 2026.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, Fox 5
